// CAPTAIN'S LOG · 05.05.2026

Tuesday, work night

Mobility, Lucy drop-off, walk after, then admin and coffee till 5pm. Tim's farm-dinner reply is the one thing that has to ship today.

DAY 1 OF 5 · WORK NIGHT 3 REPLIES TODAY ANNIVERSARY · 9 DAYS MERCATO LAUNCH · 26 DAYS FARM DINNER 1 · 11 DAYS
// LAST NIGHT · MON 05/04 · WORK NIGHT OP WIN 05/04 03:00 → 05/05 03:00 ✓
Service Recap

Solid Monday: net up, labor down, mocktails out the door.

Net Sales
$9,542
▲ +$184 · +2.0%
Covers
126
▼ -7 vs Mon
Avg Check
$170.40
▲ +$9.06
Labor (vs Net)
30.07%
▼ -0.5 pts

Comp note: last Monday (4/27) was SRW closing night, so the pasta and starter mix doesn't compare cleanly. Topline (net, check, labor) does. 7pm hour was the win — 22 transactions, $2,843 sales, 10% labor cost. 9pm tail dropped fast (2 transactions, 53% labor cost). Watch whether nights end this early consistently.

Today · Read First

Tim wants the farm-dinner PDF on one page tonight.

Mainly sunny · low 51°F

Mild dry evening. PATIO OPEN

R/O Requests

3 active · block on roster while June schedule is being built

JUN 26-28
Fri-Sun · Pride wknd
AUG 20-24
Thu-Mon
SEP 5-6
Sat-Sun

Pride weekend is the heaviest ask — three back-to-back weekend shifts. Aug 20-24 and Sep 5-6 windows fall outside active farm-dinner weekends.

JUN 24-30
Wed-Tue · 7 days

Full week off late June. Overlaps Adrian's Pride weekend ask (Jun 26-28) — both out simultaneously is a coverage gap on a peak weekend.

check sheet for cell-level entries

Sheet comment was just "R/O" with no dates specified. Cell-level R/O markers on the BOH Summer R/O Sheet need to be checked to identify the actual range.

personal-mail-summary.json is from 2026-05-03 (expected today). Midday sweep hasn't run today. Surfacing items anyway because one is deadline-today.

Service Deep Dive

Pasta drove the night, mocktails were the surprise.

Food: $5,776  (vs $5,834 last Mon, -1.0% — flat)

Top 5 by VolumeQtyRevenue
Bolognese33$1,056
Burrata26$468
Mafaldine23$598
Caesar Salad22$308
Agnolotti21$609
Top 5 by RevenueQtyRevenue
Bolognese33$1,056
Agnolotti21$609
Mafaldine23$598
Burrata26$468
Amatriciana16$448

Watch: Burrata 26 was the night's stealth driver. Tartare back on the menu post-SRW (14 / $322) — confirms Katherine's "tartare toast continued to slay" line from Sunday.
Not moving: Almonds 4, Olives 9, Endive 10, Farinata 8 — all in normal Monday range, nothing 86-worthy.

Liquor $800 · Beer $24 · Wine $1,269 · NA $246
vs last Mon (4/27): Liquor -$222 (-21.7%, post-SRW) · Wine +$217 (+20.6%) · NA +$121 (+96.8%)

NA trend: 31 items / $246, nearly double last Monday. Mocktail alone was 16 / $144 — almost 1 in 8 covers ordered one. Trend, not a problem.

Fresh Sheets + Seasonal Signals

Last call: fiddleheads, devils club, ramps. New: Local Roots Little Gem 5/6.

Fresh sheets today: none caught in this morning's inbox sweep.

Ladyfern Fiddleheads (F&F, $10.50/#)
Devils Club Shoots (F&F, $18.50/#)
Wine Cap Mushroom (F&F)
Sunchokes (Franks)
Ramps (Franks, "a few weeks more")
Nettles (Franks + F&F)
F&F flagged "last week" on fiddleheads + devils club.

Chicories ↑20.4% — Franks $29.50/cs

Fading: Stinging Nettles ~3 weeks left at F&F · Lilacs and Nettle off Kelly's 5/3 sheet · Pork Belly + Chops + Shanks last-call (May-Jun, Tim 2026 confirmed) · Treviso Radicchio supply-flagged.

Local News

Quiet news day. Restaurant scene busy, state legislature dark.

RESTAURANT
Death & Co opens at Pioneer Square (419 Occidental) this spring. Boxed wine and alcohol-free programming both forecast to keep growing in '26.
FRED HUTCH
Hutch flagged a slowdown in new-grant funding. New Sloan Precision Oncology funding window opens 5/15.
SCHOOLS
SPS kindergarten enrollment for 2026-27 still open — context for JD's path. Lucy locked at St. Catherine.
98115
NE 85th St shopping center back on the market as of Jan 2026 (long-stalled redevelopment). Otherwise quiet.
LEGIS
WA legislature adjourned 3/12; no new state bills moving. Seattle Council focused on public safety this session.
